primary_decisions_supported: Aligning sales targets with revenue timing
executive_questions_answered: How will signed bookings translate into recognized revenue over coming quarters?
Do we have a large backlog of booked deals not yet live (and why)?
Is revenue growth lagging sales bookings (indicating delays in implementation)?

metric_definitions.definition: Total annual recurring contract value signed in the period (bookings recorded when contract is signed).
metric_definitions.formula: Sum of ACV of all contracts signed in period

known_variants.definition_delta: Uses total value of contract over its term (including non-recurring elements) rather than annualized value
known_variants.when_to_use: Cash flow or billing planning when multi-year deals and one-time fees matter

pitfalls: Bookings include future revenue not yet delivered – they should not be confused with current period revenue
Renewal bookings can double-count revenue if not separated (focus new vs renewal bookings distinctly)
Cancellations or push-outs of start dates will reduce future revenue conversion – track changes to bookings

metric_definitions.definition: Percentage of booked ARR that has turned into recognized revenue in a given period. Indicates how effectively bookings are converting (e.g., implementation speed).
metric_definitions.formula: Conversion % = (Revenue recognized from prior bookings / Total prior bookings) * 100

known_variants.definition_delta: The absolute ARR value of signed deals that have not yet started billing (booked but not live)
known_variants.when_to_use: Understanding future revenue pipeline and implementation load

pitfalls: Requires mapping bookings cohorts to revenue recognition periods – inaccurate matching can misstate conversion
If many deals start much later, conversion rate will appear low (need to consider expected time lag)
Ignoring partial period revenue (pro-rated first/last month) can slightly understate conversion if not accounted

required_fields.salesforce_field: Revenue Schedule or custom Revenue__c object

joins_and_keys.notes: Link each booking to its revenue recognition schedule or invoice records

transformation_rules: Group bookings by their start date to compute backlog: if start > current quarter, it's backlog
Align recognized revenue to the originating booking (e.g., use Opportunity ID or Contract ID reference)
Exclude bookings that were canceled before start (they inflate bookings but will never convert to revenue)

data_quality_checks.logic: Sum of revenue over time from each booking should equal that booking's value (within tolerance)
Identify deals with long gaps between close and start (long implementation backlog)

derived_fields: Backlog_ARR = sum(ACV of deals signed but not started by period end)
Revenue_from_bookings = sum(revenue in period from deals closed previously)
aggregation_rules: Aggregate bookings by quarter of close, and actual revenue by quarter of recognition; then compute what % of each quarter's bookings have turned into revenue by now
Track backlog as cumulative or by aging buckets (e.g., deals booked >6 months ago not live)
calculations_and_models.cohort_logic: Use booking quarter as a cohort; monitor revenue realized from each booking cohort over subsequent quarters
calculations_and_models.forecast_logic: Predict future revenue by applying historical conversion rates or known schedules to current backlog of bookings

layout.purpose: Show bookings signed vs revenue recognized each quarter to visualize lag
Display current backlog (booked not live) and recent conversion rates of bookings to revenue

narrative_insights_to_generate: Explain if revenue shortfall is due to insufficient bookings or slower conversion of bookings to revenue
Highlight any increase in backlog suggesting implementation delays
diagnostic_questions: Are delays in onboarding causing us to push revenue into future quarters?
Is current quarter revenue mostly coming from current quarter bookings or from backlog of prior deals?

recommended_actions.action: Add implementation resources to reduce backlog
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Backlog ARR increasing QoQ or remains high relative to bookings

recommended_actions.action: Focus sales incentives on earlier start dates
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Many deals closed with long lead times to start (low near-term conversion)

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Table of quarterly bookings vs revenue and simple backlog calc to eyeball conversion
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Automated model tying each deal to a revenue schedule, with dynamic forecasts of revenue timing and backlog burn-down charts
common_client_customizations: Include billings or cash collections as an additional measure alongside revenue
Break out conversion by new vs renewal bookings (renewals often have immediate revenue vs new might have onboarding lag)
privacy_and_access_notes: Detailed contract-level revenue schedules are sensitive – restrict to Finance/RevOps; share summary conversion metrics more broadly
Ensure data aligns with official accounting schedules for external reporting
success_criteria: Accurate prediction of next quarter's revenue based on current bookings (small variance)
Reduction in backlog or improved conversion % over time if bottlenecks are addressed

references.title: Bookings vs. Revenue: Top Mistakes Companies Make When Tracking These Metrics
